channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: New Zealand’s yellow-legged hornet eradication effort shows progress, but critical containment phase remains
item.description: Six months after the discovery of a yellow-legged hornet queen in Auckland, New Zealand’s biosecurity response appears to be gaining traction, with no new nests detected since the previous month. Auth...

item.title: Asaduddin Owaisi criticises Mamata Banerjee over alleged remarks and treatment of Muslims
item.description: The requested news article could not be accessed due to a server permission restriction, and therefore the full content is not available for direct verification or detailed extraction. The link provid...

item.title: First upgraded Boeing 787-8 aircraft joins Air India fleet in Delhi after retrofit programme
item.description: Air India’s first retrofitted Boeing 787-8 aircraft, registered VT-ANT, successfully landed in Delhi at around 10:43 pm on April 13, 2026, marking a key milestone in the airline’s widebody fleet moder...

item.title: Stephen Curry returns to Warriors lineup as team looks to revive playoff hopes
item.description: Stephen Curry has returned to the Golden State Warriors’ lineup after missing more than two months due to a knee injury, bringing renewed energy and optimism to a team fighting for a place in the NBA ...

item.title: Lawmakers urged to regulate social media use among minors instead of imposing ban
item.description: A consumer advocacy group and a Philippine lawmaker are urging policymakers to focus on regulating rather than banning social media access for minors, stressing the importance of a balanced and propor...

item.title: Hungary’s political shift after Viktor Orbán’s election defeat and its global implications
item.description: Hungary has undergone a major political upheaval after voters ousted long-serving Prime Minister Viktor Orbán, ending his 16-year rule. The surprise victory of Péter Magyar and his Tisza party marks a...

item.title: Trump tips DoorDash driver during White House McDonald's delivery while promoting tax-on-tips policy
item.description: President Donald Trump hosted a DoorDash delivery driver at the White House on Monday in an event tied to his administration's push for a “no tax on tips” policy ahead of Tax Day. The driver, Sharon S...

item.title: India Resumes Iranian Oil Imports After 7 Years Using US Waiver
item.description: According to ship tracking data from LSEG, two very large crude carriers (VLCCs) loaded with Iranian oil have recently reached Indian ports, marking the first time India is receiving Iranian crude sin...

item.title: UK-Mauritius Chagos Islands deal stalled after US withdrawal of support
item.description: The proposed agreement between the United Kingdom and Mauritius over the Chagos Islands has been paused indefinitely following the withdrawal of support from US President Donald Trump, according to Fo...

item.title: Hungary election result signals setback for Europe’s illiberal right
item.description: In this opinion column, John Ivison argues that recent political developments in Hungary represent a meaningful setback for the global rise of illiberal right-wing politics. After 16 years of governan...

item.title: Police Arrest 33 Suspects Linked to Kwara Church Attack, Recover Heavy Weapons
item.description: The Nigeria Police Force has confirmed the arrest of 33 suspects believed to be part of a criminal network linked to terrorism, kidnapping, armed robbery, cattle rustling, and other violent crimes con...

item.title: US enforces Strait of Hormuz blockade amid rising tensions with Iran
item.description: The United States has begun enforcing a military blockade on ships entering or leaving Iranian ports in the Gulf and the Gulf of Oman after a deadline set by President Donald Trump expired. The move c...

item.title: Five historic Premier League title collapses highlighted amid renewed Arsenal title race concerns
item.description: The article revisits some of the most dramatic Premier League title race collapses in history, especially as Arsenal’s recent defeat to Bournemouth has raised fresh concerns among fans about their abi...

item.title: Targeted toothpaste inhibits gum disease bacteria while preserving the oral microbiome
item.description: Researchers in Germany have developed a new approach to preventing gum disease that avoids disrupting the mouth’s natural bacterial balance. Instead of relying on traditional antiseptic ingredients th...

item.title: Exploring Rocky: The Alien Engineer Who Redefined Friendship in Project Hail Mary
item.description: The article from Moss and Fog explores Rocky, the beloved alien character from Andy Weir’s novel *Project Hail Mary* and its 2026 film adaptation starring Ryan Gosling. Rocky is an Eridian engineer fr...

item.title: Police officer martyred, four injured in attack on polio vaccination security team in Hangu
item.description: A tragic incident took place in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa’s Hangu district where a police officer deployed for the protection of a polio vaccination team was martyred after unidentified armed attackers opene...

item.title: Questions raised over legality of US Marines involvement in Cape Town Metro Police fitness session
item.description: Concerns have been raised following the appearance of United States Marines conducting physical training exercises with Cape Town Metro Police cadets on Muizenberg Beach. Images and videos of the sess...

item.title: Canadian Government Considers Social Media Restrictions for Minors
item.description: Concerns surrounding the impact of social media on youth in Canada are prompting a national discussion about potential restrictions. The current debate centers on a possible ban for underage users, sp...

item.title: Two sisters die in tragic motorcycle accident due to broken road patch in Karachi
item.description: In Karachi’s Nazimabad No 1 area, a tragic accident late Monday night claimed the lives of two young sisters when their motorcycle slipped on a damaged section of the road. The bike lost balance as th...

item.title: Man sentenced to community service for selling counterfeit Oasis tickets online
item.description: A man has been sentenced to a community order after admitting to selling counterfeit tickets for Oasis reunion concerts online. The fraudster reportedly deceived fans by advertising tickets for events...
